name: Chicago Deep Dish Pizza Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast and sugar in 1/4 cup of the water until foamy.
2. In a large bowl, combine 2 3/4 cups flour, cornmeal, salt, oil, and remaining 3/4 cup water.
3. Stir in the yeast mixture vigorously.
4. Turn dough out onto a floured surface and knead smooth and elastic, adding more flour if necessary to create a springy dough.
5. Let rise double in an oiled bowl.
6. Punch down and push dough into a deep dish pan or 12 to 15 inch pizza pan.
7. The dough will continue to rise as you prepare the toppings.
8. Top as desired.
9. Bake in a preheated 500°F oven for 15 minutes.
10. Lower heat to 375°F and bake until bottom of crust is light brown, about another 25 minutes or so.
11. Serve immediately.
Note: For my abm (automatic bread maker), I use 1 cup water, 1/4 cup oil (olive preferred), 3 cups flour, 1/2 cup cornmeal, 1 tsp salt, a tsp sugar, and 2 tsp yeast. Follow your manufacturer's instructions to make dough in your abm. If you don't use the dough immediately, you can freeze it or refrigerate it overnight in a large floured plastic bag. Punch down the dough now and then as needed. Enjoy!
